content:
over on the rust zulip we're working on a plan for coming up with a stable replacement for #[may_dangle] and are wondering if the Linux Kernel would have any requirements or use cases?
https://rust-lang.zulipchat.com/#narrow/stream/213817-t-lang/topic/deprecating.20spooky-dropck-at-a-distance/near/327679698
e.g. allowing structs with self-references (the &'a variety, not just raw pointers) to be stored in rbtree or other containers defined by Linux

We the following code in the rust branch, but these are not in linux mainline yet:

https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/blob/rust/rust/kernel/linked_list.rs
https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/blob/rust/rust/kernel/rbtree.rs
(Thanks a lot, by the way, for contacting us!)
I think we don't use #[may_dangel] today because our List<T>::drop() does touch something in T (actually T is T: GetLinks, which implies there is a list entry inside T).
